description: Migrate all MCP prompt workflows and resource guides from the MCP server into .claude/skills/ SKILL.md files, then remove prompt/resource capabilities from the MCP server entirely. The MCP server should only serve tools — all workflow guidance lives in skills that AI agents load on demand via the native skill tool. Update SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS to reference skills instead of prompts/resources.

userStories.title: Create build-plugin skill from build_plugin prompt + plugin-development + sdk-api resources
userStories.description: Create .claude/skills/build-plugin/SKILL.md and __SKILL__.md that combines the build_plugin prompt workflow (7 phases), the plugin-development resource (architecture, SDK, patterns, auth, lifecycle hooks), and the sdk-api resource (full API reference) into a single comprehensive skill.
acceptanceCriteria: .claude/skills/build-plugin/SKILL.md exists with correct frontmatter (name: build-plugin, description mentioning plugin development)
.claude/skills/build-plugin/__SKILL__.md contains the full build_plugin 7-phase workflow, all 20 gotchas, code templates (API wrapper, tool pattern, plugin class), and Phase 7 learnings contribution

__SKILL__.md also contains the plugin-development resource content (architecture, plugin structure, SDK utilities, ToolError, Zod schema rules, auth patterns, lifecycle hooks) and the sdk-api reference (OpenTabsPlugin, defineTool, ToolError factories, DOM/fetch/storage/timing utilities)
.claude/skills/build-plugin/CLAUDE.md explains the stub/content separation pattern
The skill tool's available_skills block shows build-plugin with its description
userStories.notes:
Files:
- .claude/skills/build-plugin/SKILL.md (new — stub with frontmatter)
- .claude/skills/build-plugin/__SKILL__.md (new — full content)
- .claude/skills/build-plugin/CLAUDE.md (new — maintenance guide)

Context:
The build_plugin prompt text is in `platform/mcp-server/src/prompts/build-plugin.ts` (exported as `buildPluginPromptText(url, name)`). It has 7 phases: prerequisites, codebase research, web app exploration (with browser tools), scaffolding, tool design, implementation (API wrapper + tool patterns), build/test + mandatory learnings. It also has 20 gotchas.

The plugin-development resource is in `platform/mcp-server/src/resources/plugin-development.ts` (~596 lines). It covers architecture, plugin structure, package.json opentabs field, OpenTabsPlugin class, defineTool factory, SDK utilities, ToolError, Zod schema rules, auth patterns (cookie, JWT, globalThis persistence), lifecycle hooks, and advanced patterns.

The sdk-api resource is in `platform/mcp-server/src/resources/sdk-api.ts` (~210 lines). It covers the full SDK API reference.

Combine all three into __SKILL__.md. The prompt text has URL-specific placeholders (`${url}`, `${name}`) — replace these with generic placeholders like `<target-url>` and `<plugin-name>` since skills are not parameterized. Remove the `contribute_learnings` references from Phase 7 — instead, tell the agent to update the skill file directly if it discovers new patterns.

Follow the exact same stub/content pattern as .claude/skills/ralph/ — SKILL.md is a stub with frontmatter that tells the AI to read __SKILL__.md, and CLAUDE.md explains the pattern.

Frontmatter triggers should include: build plugin, create plugin, develop plugin, new plugin, plugin development.

userStories.title: Create plugin-icon skill from plugin_icon prompt
userStories.description: Create .claude/skills/plugin-icon/SKILL.md and __SKILL__.md from the plugin_icon prompt content (icon pipeline, SVG requirements, preparation steps, Simple Icons fallback).
acceptanceCriteria: .claude/skills/plugin-icon/SKILL.md exists with correct frontmatter (name: plugin-icon)

.claude/skills/plugin-icon/__SKILL__.md contains the full icon workflow: pipeline explanation, SVG requirements table, obtaining SVGs, preparation (square viewBox, cleanup, file size), placement, build, verification
.claude/skills/plugin-icon/CLAUDE.md explains the stub/content pattern
userStories.notes:
Files:
- .claude/skills/plugin-icon/SKILL.md (new)
- .claude/skills/plugin-icon/__SKILL__.md (new)
- .claude/skills/plugin-icon/CLAUDE.md (new)

Context:
The plugin_icon prompt text is in `platform/mcp-server/src/prompts/plugin-icon.ts` (exported as `pluginIconPromptText(plugin)`). It covers the icon pipeline (icon.svg → opentabs-plugin build → tools.json → side panel), SVG requirements (viewBox square, no explicit width/height, no <image>/<script>), dark mode auto-generation, obtaining SVGs from brand pages or Simple Icons, preparation (square viewBox, cleanup, file size), placement, build, and verification.

Replace `${plugin}` with `<plugin-name>` since skills are not parameterized.

Remove the 'Write Learnings Back' section at the end — instead tell the agent to update this skill file directly.

Frontmatter triggers: plugin icon, add icon, update icon, svg icon.

userStories.title: Create troubleshoot skill from troubleshoot prompt + troubleshooting resource
userStories.description: Create .claude/skills/troubleshoot/SKILL.md and __SKILL__.md combining the troubleshoot prompt (9-step diagnostic workflow) with the troubleshooting resource (error reference, diagnostic tools, resolution steps).
acceptanceCriteria: .claude/skills/troubleshoot/SKILL.md exists with correct frontmatter (name: troubleshoot)

.claude/skills/troubleshoot/__SKILL__.md contains the 9-step diagnostic workflow (extension connectivity, plugin state, permissions, timeout, rate limiting, tool-not-found, logs, browser diagnostics) and the error reference table with all 11 error types
.claude/skills/troubleshoot/CLAUDE.md explains the stub/content pattern
userStories.notes:
Files:
- .claude/skills/troubleshoot/SKILL.md (new)
- .claude/skills/troubleshoot/__SKILL__.md (new)
- .claude/skills/troubleshoot/CLAUDE.md (new)

Context:
The troubleshoot prompt text is in `platform/mcp-server/src/prompts/troubleshoot.ts` (exported as `troubleshootPromptText(error)`). It has 9 steps: check extension connectivity, check plugin state/tab readiness, check permissions, check timeout, check rate limiting, check tool-not-found, inspect logs, browser diagnostics, write learnings.

The troubleshooting resource is in `platform/mcp-server/src/resources/troubleshooting.ts` (~192 lines). It contains an error reference with 11 error types (each with symptoms, likely cause, resolution, and example error message), plus diagnostic tools and resolution steps.

Combine both into __SKILL__.md. Replace `${error}` with generic text. Remove 'Write Learnings Back' section — tell the agent to update this skill file directly.

Frontmatter triggers: troubleshoot, debug, fix, not working, error, broken.

userStories.title: Create setup-plugin skill from setup_plugin prompt + quick-start resource
userStories.description: Create .claude/skills/setup-plugin/SKILL.md and __SKILL__.md combining the setup_plugin prompt (8-step installation workflow) with the quick-start resource (installation, configuration, first tool call).
acceptanceCriteria: .claude/skills/setup-plugin/SKILL.md exists with correct frontmatter (name: setup-plugin)
.claude/skills/setup-plugin/__SKILL__.md contains the 8-step setup workflow (search, install, open app, verify loaded, review, test, configure permissions, summary) and the quick-start content
.claude/skills/setup-plugin/CLAUDE.md explains the stub/content pattern
userStories.notes:
Files:
- .claude/skills/setup-plugin/SKILL.md (new)
- .claude/skills/setup-plugin/__SKILL__.md (new)
- .claude/skills/setup-plugin/CLAUDE.md (new)

Context:
The setup_plugin prompt text is in `platform/mcp-server/src/prompts/setup-plugin.ts` (exported as `setupPluginPromptText(name)`). It has 8 steps: search npm, install, open web app, verify loaded, review (inspect + mark_reviewed), test, configure permissions, write learnings.

The quick-start resource is in `platform/mcp-server/src/resources/quick-start.ts` (~210 lines). It covers installation, MCP client configuration (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, generic), plugin discovery, first tool call, permissions, and common issues.

Combine both into __SKILL__.md. Replace `${pluginName}` and `${packageName}` with `<plugin-name>` and `<package-name>`. Remove 'Write Learnings Back' section.

Frontmatter triggers: setup plugin, install plugin, enable plugin, configure plugin, add plugin.

userStories.title: Remove all MCP prompts from the server
userStories.description: Delete all prompt-related files from the MCP server: the prompt registry, all prompt content files, and remove prompt capability declaration and handlers from mcp-setup.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-prompts.ts is deleted
platform/mcp-server/src/prompts/ directory and all files within it are deleted
mcp-setup.ts no longer declares prompts capability
mcp-setup.ts no longer registers ListPromptsRequestSchema or GetPromptRequestSchema handlers
No imports of prompt-related modules remain in platform/mcp-server/src/
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-prompts.ts (delete)
- platform/mcp-server/src/prompts/build-plugin.ts (delete)
- platform/mcp-server/src/prompts/troubleshoot.ts (delete)
- platform/mcp-server/src/prompts/setup-plugin.ts (delete)
- platform/mcp-server/src/prompts/plugin-icon.ts (delete)
- platform/mcp-server/src/prompts/audit-ai-docs.ts (delete)
- platform/mcp-server/src/prompts/contribute-learnings.ts (delete)
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.ts (modify — remove prompt capability + handlers)

Context:
In `mcp-setup.ts`:
- Remove `prompts: { listChanged: true }` from the capabilities object (line ~391)
- Remove the `ListPromptsRequestSchema` handler registration (line ~197)
- Remove the `GetPromptRequestSchema` handler registration (line ~207)
- Remove imports of `getAllPromptsList` and `resolvePrompt` from `./mcp-prompts.js`

In `mcp-prompts.ts` (line ~1-325): This file imports from all 6 prompt content files and exports `getAllPromptsList()` and `resolvePrompt()`. Delete the entire file.

Delete the entire `platform/mcp-server/src/prompts/` directory.

After deletion, grep the entire `platform/mcp-server/src/` directory for any remaining references to the deleted modules.

Constraints:
- Do NOT remove the `opentabs://status` dynamic resource yet — that's handled in the next story
- Do NOT modify SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS yet — that's a separate story

userStories.title: Update SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS to reference skills instead of prompts/resources
userStories.description: Rewrite the SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS constant in mcp-setup.ts to remove all references to MCP prompts, resources, and the contribute_learnings workflow. Add a brief section about skills being the mechanism for workflow guidance.
acceptanceCriteria: SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS no longer mentions prompts/get, resources/read, or any opentabs:// URIs
SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS no longer contains the 'Workflow Triggers' table
SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS no longer contains the 'Contributing Learnings' section
SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS no longer contains the 'Resources' section listing URIs
SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS contains a brief 'Workflow Guidance' section telling the AI to use the skill tool for tasks like building plugins, troubleshooting, setting up plugins, and adding icons
SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS is under 80 lines (slimmed down from ~98)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.ts (modify SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS constant, lines 281-378)

Context:
The current SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS (~98 lines) contains these sections that must be removed or replaced:
1. 'Contributing Learnings (Mandatory — Do This After Every Task)' (lines 345-351) — DELETE entirely. Skills handle their own documentation.
2. 'Workflow Triggers' table (lines 353-364) — REPLACE with a brief 'Workflow Guidance' section that says: 'For complex tasks like building plugins, troubleshooting issues, setting up plugins, or adding plugin icons, use the `skill` tool to load the relevant skill — these contain accumulated patterns and step-by-step workflows.'
3. 'Resources' section (lines 368-378) listing all opentabs:// URIs — DELETE entirely.

Keep these sections unchanged:
- Tool Categories
- Security Rules (all 4)
- Plugin Review Flow
- Multi-Tab Targeting
- Permission States
- Error Handling

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the security rules
- Do NOT change the tool categories section
- The instructions must remain concise — under 80 lines

userStories.title: Delete MCP prompts E2E test file
userStories.description: Delete the e2e/mcp-prompts.e2e.ts test file since MCP prompts no longer exist.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/mcp-prompts.e2e.ts is deleted
No imports or references to mcp-prompts.e2e.ts remain in e2e/
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/mcp-prompts.e2e.ts (delete)

Context:
This file contains 5 tests for MCP prompts (list, get with url, get with url+name, get with defaults, error for unknown prompt, lifecycle test). Since we removed all prompts from the server, these tests will fail. Delete the file.

Also check e2e/fixtures.ts for any prompt-related helper functions or types that might need cleanup. Check if there are any references to 'listPrompts' or 'getPrompt' in fixtures.ts — if so, the methods can stay since they're part of the MCP client library, but any test-specific helpers should be removed.

Constraints:
- Do NOT delete or modify any other E2E test files
- Do NOT remove MCP client methods (listPrompts, getPrompt) from fixtures if they come from the MCP SDK — only remove test-specific helpers

userStories.title: Update root CLAUDE.md to reference skills for workflow guidance
userStories.description: Update the root CLAUDE.md to add a brief section about skills being available for plugin development, troubleshooting, etc. Remove any references to MCP prompts or resources as workflow delivery mechanisms.
acceptanceCriteria: CLAUDE.md contains a section mentioning that skills exist for plugin development, troubleshooting, plugin setup, and plugin icons
CLAUDE.md does not reference MCP prompts/get or resources/read as mechanisms for workflow guidance
Any 'Workflow Triggers' section added by the previous ralph PRD (commit a137929e) is removed or replaced with the skills reference
userStories.notes:
Files:
- CLAUDE.md (modify)

Context:
The root CLAUDE.md may contain a 'MCP Workflow Triggers' section added by commit a137929e (from the ralph-2026-03-07-180630-mcp-workflow-accessibility-a7ee81 branch). Check if this section exists on the current main branch. If it does, replace it. If it doesn't (the branch wasn't merged), add a new brief section.

Add something like:

```
### Workflow Skills

For complex tasks, use the `skill` tool to load step-by-step workflows:

- **build-plugin**: Full plugin development workflow (7 phases, auth discovery, API mapping, implementation patterns)
- **plugin-icon**: Adding or updating SVG icons for plugins
- **troubleshoot**: Diagnosing platform issues (extension connectivity, permissions, timeouts)
- **setup-plugin**: Installing and configuring existing plugins from npm
```

Place this in the 'Key Concepts' or 'You Are Connected to This Project's MCP Server' section where it's natural.

Constraints:
- Keep the addition brief (5-10 lines)
- Do NOT enumerate individual plugins
- Do NOT add excessive detail — the skills themselves contain the detail
